title: Determination of Oxalates by Oxidation with NonAqueous Iodine Solution
author: G. P. SONI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Post-Graduate Studies and Research in Chemistry, University of Jabalpur, Jabalpur, (M.P.) 

  Manuscript received 31 July 1969 ; accepted 2 November 1973  

 A titrimetric method for the determination of oxalates has been described involving oxidation with iodine dissolved in carbon tetrachloride. The procedure is applicable for the evaluation of S to 40 mg. of oxalate ion with an accuracy of ± 0.5%. With slight modification the proposed procedure can be adopted far estimating oxalates in presence of chloride ion.
